emails.preview_text: March just showed up. Someone in your life needs to feel that.
emails.body_text: It's the first of March. A new month is basically a free reset — and there's someone in your life who could use a reminder that new beginnings are real. You don't need a reason to send a vibe check. A new month is reason enough. Go send one.

emails.subject: The text that changed her whole afternoon
emails.preview_text: It was three words. That's it.
emails.body_text: She'd had one of those invisible-hard days. Nothing catastrophic — just the kind of day where everything feels slightly off and nobody notices. Then her phone buzzed. An old coworker had sent her a vibe check card that said "thinking of you today." She cried a little. Then felt better. Three words from someone who barely knew her day was hard. You have that power too — go use it.
emails.body_html:
✨ The Vibe Check

She cried a little. Then felt better.

emails.preview_text: Showing up when it's hard is underrated.

The fact that you showed up today — even when part of you didn't want to — is not a small thing. That's resilience. That's character. Most people don't see it, but it's real and it matters.

Know someone else who's showing up quietly? Let them know you see it.

emails.subject: He almost didn't send it
emails.preview_text: He thought it would seem weird. He sent it anyway.
emails.body_text: He'd been thinking about his dad for weeks — just random flashes of memory, things his dad used to say. He almost talked himself out of reaching out because "it would seem weird out of nowhere." He sent a vibe check anyway. His dad called him that same evening and they talked for an hour. Sometimes weird-out-of-nowhere is exactly the right move.

emails.subject: She sent the card she kept putting off
emails.preview_text: She'd been meaning to for months. Today was finally the day.
emails.body_text: She'd been meaning to reach out to her college friend for months. Life got busy. Then a little awkward. Then it felt too late to just text out of nowhere. She sent a vibe check card with nothing but "thinking about you" and a sunshine theme. Her friend replied in minutes: "I needed this so bad today." Nothing is ever too late to send.

emails.subject: The birthday she thought no one remembered
emails.preview_text: Three words showed up in her inbox at 8am.
emails.body_text: Her birthday was on a Wednesday. She hadn't posted about it, hadn't mentioned it at work. She figured it would just pass quietly. Then at 8am a vibe check card landed in her inbox from an old coworker: "Happy birthday, queen." She screenshot it. She still has it. Three words. Completely changed her day. Someone in your life has a birthday coming up. You know who.

emails.subject: The roommate she almost didn't message
emails.preview_text: They hadn't talked in two years. She almost talked herself out of it.
emails.body_text: They'd been roommates for two years in college, drifted apart after graduation, and hadn't spoken since. She thought about reaching out a dozen times but always talked herself out of it — too much time had passed, too weird. She sent a vibe check card. Just: "Hey, I was thinking about you. Hope you're doing great." Her old roommate replied with a voice note that lasted four minutes. Some connections don't expire. They just wait.
emails.body_html:
✨ The Vibe Check

They'd been roommates for two years in college, drifted apart after graduation, and hadn't spoken since.

emails.subject: The one she sent at midnight
emails.preview_text: Couldn't sleep. Sent a vibe. Changed everything.
emails.body_text: She couldn't sleep. 12:47am, scrolling her phone, thinking about her mom — who lived alone, who never complained, who always said she was fine. She sent a vibe check card right then. Just: "I love you. I don't say it enough." Her mom called the next morning and said she'd been crying happy tears. Sometimes the best time to send a vibe check is right when you think of it.
emails.body_html:

Send this reminder to someone who needs to hear it too.

Nobody has it fully figured out. Not one person. The people who look the most certain are just better at not saying it out loud. It's okay that you're still figuring it out — that means you're still growing, still curious, still open. That's a good thing to be.

Send this permission to someone who's been hard on themselves lately.

emails.subject: The one that saved Sunday
emails.preview_text: Sunday dread is real. One card fixed the whole day.
emails.body_text: Sunday afternoon. That familiar dread settling in before the week starts. She'd been sitting with it for two hours when a vibe check card popped up from her best friend: "You've got this week. I know it." That was it. That was the whole message. But it was enough to shift everything. She closed her laptop, made tea, and felt genuinely okay. Sometimes one sentence from the right person is better than any motivation content you could find.
emails.body_html:
✨ The Vibe Check

Sunday afternoon. That familiar dread settling in before the week starts.

She'd been sitting with it for two hours when a vibe check card popped up from her best friend: "You've got this week. I know it."

That was it. That was the whole message. But it was enough to shift everything. She closed her laptop, made tea, and felt genuinely okay.

Sometimes one sentence from the right person is better than any motivation content you could find.
